response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
  "opinion": "I've been dealing with this issue for years now, watching as so much misinformation spreads online. As a woman from Naples who's married with one child but unemployed right now, I spend only occasional time on social media—maybe once or twice a month—but that doesn't mean the fake content doesn't affect me when I'm there. It can be overwhelming, especially since I have depression and sometimes seeing all this nonsense just makes me feel more anxious.",
  "reasoning_summary": "I considered my personal background first: being in Naples, Italy with limited education and a depressed state might make me particularly vulnerable to fake news because it affects how I process information.   "tone": "Concerned yet pragmatic",
  "citations": [],

}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: I've been dealing with this issue for years now, watching as so much misinformation spreads online. As a woman from Naples who's married with one child but unemployed right now, I spend only occasional time on social media—maybe once or twice a month—but that doesn't mean the fake content doesn't affect me when I'm there. It can be overwhelming, especially since I have depression and sometimes seeing all this nonsense just makes me feel more anxious.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I considered my personal background first: being in Naples, Italy with limited education and a depressed state might make me particularly vulnerable to fake news because it affects how I process information. Then, looking at the ocean traits—high extraversion means I enjoy connecting with people but low conscientiousness makes me less likely to fact-check rigorously.
